functions.name: u2flib_host.u2f_v2.register
u2flib_host.u2f_v2.authenticate
documentation:
Signs an authentication challenge

data = {
    'version': "U2F_V2",
    'challenge': websafe_encode(self.challenge),
    'appId': self.binding.app_id,
    'keyHandle': websafe_encode(self.binding.key_handle)
}

This simulates the U2F browser API with a soft U2F device connected.

It can be used for testing.

classes.name: u2flib_host.device.U2FDevice

methods.name: u2flib_host.device.U2FDevice.open
documentation: Opens the device for use.

methods.name: u2flib_host.device.U2FDevice.close
documentation: Closes the device, making it available for use by others.

methods.name: u2flib_host.device.U2FDevice.get_supported_versions
documentation: Gets a list of supported U2F versions from the device.

methods.name: u2flib_host.device.U2FDevice._do_send_apdu
documentation: Sends an APDU to the device, and returns the response.

methods.name: u2flib_host.device.U2FDevice.send_apdu
documentation: Sends an APDU to the device, and waits for a response.

A handle to a U2F device.
device.open() needs to be called prior to using the device, and
device.close() should be called when the device is no longer needed, to
ensure that any held resources are released.
As an aternative, the 'with' statement can be used:

    with device as dev:
        dev.send_apdu(...)
